reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: the teaching profession and a child-centred public school
reactions.public_good_frame: a way out of the education crisis exists and is written down
reactions.argument: A fairer gate on the same building is progress, but the documented reform trajectory this archetype draws on aims to need no gate before 14 at all.
reactions.condition_to_change: Only as an explicit transition step towards the comprehensive model.

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: the teaching profession and a child-centred public school
reactions.public_good_frame: a way out of the education crisis exists and is written down
reactions.argument: The documented reform proposal this archetype draws on proposes exactly this: transferring the 6/8-year forms into the comprehensive model gradually, with broad social consensus.
reactions.condition_to_change: Gradualism must have a fixed end-state, not become permanent limbo.

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: the teaching profession and a child-centred public school
reactions.public_good_frame: a way out of the education crisis exists and is written down
reactions.argument: The documented programme this archetype draws on calls for unified general education as long as possible — at least 10 years — because the 6/8 forms are 'a tool of elite social separation'.
reactions.condition_to_change: Bundle with teacher-support systems, as the Finnish lesson requires.

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: the teaching profession and a child-centred public school
reactions.public_good_frame: a way out of the education crisis exists and is written down
reactions.argument: This archetype also calls for stronger general schools — but as the floor of structural reform, not its substitute.
reactions.condition_to_change: Acceptable only alongside a stated structural trajectory.
